description_long: This object is a bright red windsock with a distinctive arrow-shaped design, featuring a wide, flat, V-shaped tail and a long, tapered body ending in a pointed tip. The main body appears to be constructed from a lightweight, rigid material, likely metal or plastic, with a smooth, glossy finish. At the base of the V-tail, there is a central mounting hub, which includes a visible pivot or swivel mechanism for wind alignment. The tail section is broad and flat, providing high visibility and stability in the wind, while the pointed front section is streamlined for minimal air resistance. The mounting hub is attached to a vertical support spike, suggesting it is designed to be pole-mounted. The overall appearance is functional and robust, suitable for outdoor installation in environments where wind direction needs to be clearly indicated.
description: A bright red, arrow-shaped windsock with a wide V-tail, pointed front, and central mounting hub, designed for outdoor wind direction indication. The object features a glossy, smooth finish and a visible support spike for pole mounting.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, V-shaped tail and central mounting hub.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the flat, broad tail, pointed front, and vertical support spike.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the narrow, pointed body and the full width of the V-tail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the tail cutout and mounting spike.
